errors.apiUnreachableBody: The app couldn't connect to the server. Check that it's running and try again.

llm.pickToStart: Pick an AI model to start
llm.modelOptionalHint: Optional — leave empty for the provider default
llm.modelInvalidHelp: This may be a model name issue. Check the provider's official model list:
llm.modelNameFormatHint: The name must match exactly — e.g. {{example}}. Even one wrong or missing character will fail.
llm.modelDefaultHint: Or clear the model field to use that provider's default model.
llm.mergnBuiltIn: MergN's built-in model. No API key needed — usage counts toward your plan.
llm.ownKeyHint: Your own key — usage is billed to you, not counted toward your plan limits.
llm.weakLocalTitle: ⚠ This model looks too weak for the builder
llm.weakLocalStructuredWrong: Produced structured output but it was wrong (didn't follow the instruction exactly).
llm.weakLocalNoSchema: Could not produce the required JSON schema.
llm.weakLocalAdvice: The builder generates every step with forced schema; small/local models often can't. Stronger models recommended: Llama 3.1 70B / Qwen 2.5 32B+ or a cloud model (Gemini / GPT-4o / Claude).
llm.weakCloudBody: The provider didn't respond — likely a key, quota, or billing issue.
llm.probeSuccess: ✓ Model passed structured output test ({{ms}} ms)
llm.planLockedBody: You're on <bold>MergN</bold>, the built-in model. Upgrade to Pro to use your own model and API key — your own usage isn't counted toward your plan limits.

auth.signinSubtitle: Sign in to save and run your workflows.
auth.signupSubtitle: Create an account to keep building.
auth.name: Name
auth.toSignup: Don't have an account? Sign up
auth.toSignin: Already have an account? Sign in

landing.heroTitle: Describe what should happen. MergN runs it.
landing.heroSubtitle: MergN builds the steps from your description, runs them in production, and keeps every run in view. When something fails, it finds the cause and proposes a fix — you approve every change.

differentiators.title: Built for running work, not wiring flows
differentiators.subtitle: MergN turns what you describe into a system you can see, operate, and trust in production.

generated.title: No templates
generated.desc: No pre-built workflows to pick from. Just describe your problem, and MergN builds the steps around your exact case instead of forcing you into a generic template.

visible.title: Every step is real code
visible.desc: Click any step to see the JavaScript behind it, and tweak it if you want. You always see what's actually running, nothing hidden in the background.

resilient.title: Resilient in production
resilient.desc: When a run fails, MergN figures out what went wrong and suggests a fix. You review and approve every change before it goes live.

selfHeal.title: When a run fails, MergN helps you fix it
selfHeal.subtitle: MergN catches the failure, finds the cause, and proposes a fix. Applying it is entirely up to you.
selfHeal.detectDesc: MergN notices a run has failed.
selfHeal.diagnoseDesc: It finds the root cause of the failure.
selfHeal.fixDesc: It proposes a fix — applying it is up to you.
selfHeal.diagnosis: post_discord received an empty 'message' — the upstream mapping was wrong
selfHeal.controlBanner: Nothing is applied until you approve it.
selfHeal.guardrails: Even in auto mode the guardrails apply: blast radius, loop limit, and kill switch.

showcase.clickStep: Click a step to see its code

useCases.title: What can you run?
useCases.subtitle: Describe the outcome — MergN figures out the steps and keeps them running.

payments.title: Payment alerts
payments.desc: Post a rich alert to Slack the moment a Stripe payment succeeds.

orders.title: Order notifications
orders.desc: Announce every new Shopify order in your Discord community.

leads.title: Lead capture
leads.desc: Send new Typeform submissions straight into HubSpot as contacts.

support.title: Support triage
support.desc: Turn incoming Gmail requests into tracked Linear tickets.

incidents.title: Incident escalation
incidents.desc: Route critical Datadog alerts to the on-call Slack channel.

digests.title: Daily digests
digests.desc: Summarize activity on a schedule and save it to Notion.

approvals.title: Approval routing
approvals.desc: Send Airtable approval requests to Slack and capture the decision instantly.

reports.title: Weekly reports
reports.desc: Take weekly summaries from Google Sheets and post them to Discord automatically.

community.title: Join us as we build what's next.
community.desc: Jump into Discord for direct feedback, early feature access, and fast support as MergN grows.

chat.payment1: When a payment succeeds in Stripe, post a rich alert to our Discord channel.
chat.payment2: Also include the customer's email and only alert for charges over $100.
chat.invoice1: Every morning, find overdue Stripe invoices, nudge customers, then summarize it in Slack.
chat.invoice2: Include the invoice due date and total amount in each reminder.
chat.order1: On a new Stripe order, tell fulfillment in Slack and celebrate the sale in Discord.
chat.order2: Mention the customer's first name in the Discord message.
chat.incident1: Triage monitoring alerts and escalate the critical ones to Slack and Discord.
chat.incident2: Only ping Discord when the incident is marked critical.
chat.refund1: When Stripe issues a refund, alert support in Slack and log it to Discord.
chat.refund2: Include the refund reason if Stripe provides one.

logsPanel.emptyHint: Build, run, and connection errors show up here.

chat.emptyTitle: Build a workflow in plain language
chat.emptySubtitle: Describe what should happen. I'll wire up the trigger and steps for you.

example.stripeSlack: When a Stripe payment succeeds, send a receipt to Slack
example.digest: Summarize new signups and email me a daily digest
example.github: On a new GitHub issue, post a triage note to Discord

connections.title: Connections
connections.noCode: No client code for this provider.

pipeline.describe: Describe an automation in the chat to build a pipeline.

story.title: Your workflow, in plain language
story.describe: Describe an automation in the chat — it appears here as readable steps you can follow end to end.

run.resumeTitle: Re-run from the failed step, reusing earlier outputs
run.makeFormTitle: Generate an input form from the trigger fields
run.saveForHistory: Save the workflow to keep a history of its runs.
run.selectStepCode: Select a step to see its generated code.
run.emptyState: Run the workflow to see each step's state here.
run.noFiles: No files uploaded yet — upload one in the Files tab.

node.selectStep: Select a step to see its details, settings, and generated code.
node.needsValue: Needs a value — fill it in the Input tab
node.conditional: Conditional — runs only when its condition is met

connectionDialog.name: Name
connectionDialog.nameHelp: A label to tell this apart from other connections, e.g. "work" or "test".
connectionDialog.redirectInfo: You'll be redirected to {{name}} to authorize access.
connectionDialog.redirectInfoScope: You'll be redirected to {{name}} to authorize access with scope {{scopes}}.
connectionDialog.noOAuthApp: No OAuth app is configured for {{name}}. Register one (redirect URL {{url}}), then paste its credentials.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.subtitle: how this workflow starts
trigger.webhookInfo: Send a POST here; the JSON body becomes the trigger output.
trigger.saveFirst: Save the workflow first to get its webhook URL.
trigger.applyHint: Save the workflow to apply this trigger.

desc.manual: Run by hand from the Run panel.
desc.webhook: Run when an HTTP POST hits a URL.
desc.poll: Check a service periodically for new data.
desc.event: Run on an event from a connected app.

files.emptyHint: Drag & drop or “Upload”. Your flows and the AI can access these files.

mcp.title: Connect Claude / ChatGPT
mcp.subtitle: Drive your workflows from an AI chat app — it builds and runs them for you.
mcp.chatHelp: In your chat app open Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector, paste the URL above, then Connect and approve. No token needed — you sign in with your account.
mcp.cliHelp: Generate a token below, then run this in your terminal:
mcp.tokensHelp: For CLI clients that don't do the sign-in flow. The token is shown once.
mcp.tokenOnce: Copy this now — it won't be shown again.
mcp.planLocked: Remote MCP is available on paid plans. Upgrade to connect.
mcp.error: Could not create token. Please try again.

monitoring.settingsSoon: Editing these lands with the settings milestone.
monitoring.alertsToggleHint: Off by default. When on, this flow's errors/recoveries notify your channels and run alert-handler flows. (The activity log records them either way.)
monitoring.alertChannelsSoon: Choosing destinations (Discord, Slack, email) and per-severity rules is coming soon. For now the toggle controls all configured channels.

heal.noFixes: No fixes yet for this flow.

versions.title: Version history
versions.label: Label (optional)
versions.noChange: No changes since the last version.
versions.empty: No versions yet — save one above.

review.title: Change review
review.pickNodeHint: Click a highlighted node to see its change.

settings.title: Flow settings
settings.healHint: Auto-diagnose + fix when this flow fails
settings.modeHint: What happens when a fix is found
settings.autoReplayHint: Replay buffered events after a successful fix
settings.alertsHint: Send external alerts for this flow

governance.title: Governance
governance.killHint: Instantly stop all automatic fixes across every flow

alert.title: Alert channels
alert.label: Label (optional)
alert.noHandlers: None — give a flow a 'monitor' trigger to handle alerts
alert.handlersHint: A flow with a 'monitor' trigger auto-runs on every alert, receiving the event as its input — run custom logic instead of (or with) a channel.

doctor.emptySubtitle: Ask about this flow's health, diagnose a failure, or have me fix it.
doctor.placeholder: Ask the Doctor about this flow…

suggest.lastFail: Why did the last run fail?
